question: Relay FDV above $900M one day after launch?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Relay's governance token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

The FDV will be determined using the total token supply multiplied by the token price.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Relay (https://x.com/Relayprotocol) doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

question: Obras vs. San Lorenzo
description:
In the upcoming LNB game, scheduled for February 12 at 6:30PM ET:
If the Obras win, the market will resolve to "Obras".
If the San Lorenzo win, the market will resolve to "San Lorenzo".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

eventMetadata.context_description: San Lorenzo's edge as slight favorites at 55% implied probability stems from their stronger recent form in the Argentine LNB, where they've won four of their last five games, including a key victory over a top contender, while Obras Sanitarias has dropped three straight amid defensive lapses. Head-to-head history favors San Lorenzo, who have claimed seven of the last 10 meetings, often dominating rebounding and paint scoring. No major injuries reported on either side per official updates, but San Lorenzo benefits from home-court rest advantage and deeper bench rotation. Trader consensus reflects these dynamics, though Obras' occasional upset potential adds volatility to the matchup.

question: Will 100 Thieves qualify to a S-tier tournament by June 1 ?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if 100 Thieves' CS2 roster qualifies for (i.e., participates in the main event/group stage of) any S-Tier CS2 tournament that concludes by June 1, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purpose of this market, an "S-Tier Event" refers to any tournament classified as S-Tier on Liquipedia.net. Online-only events, or events downgraded prior to event start (e.g., downgraded to A-Tier due to team attendance) do not qualify. If an S-Tier event is downgraded after it has concluded, this event will still qualify as an "S-Tier Event".

Qualification is determined by the team appearing in the main tournament bracket or group stage as listed on Liquipedia.net (not merely being invited to qualifiers or play-ins unless they advance to the S-Tier main event).

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the tournament organizers (e.g., ESL, PGL, BLAST.tv, etc.)

The competitive balance stems from a stacked NA field, with teams like G2 Esports and Leviatán dominating group stages and playoffs, while 100T's path hinges on winning the upcoming Challengers Playoffs and Last Chance Qualifier amid roster chemistry issues post-offseason tweaks. A hot streak in next week's decisive series could surge "Yes" odds; conversely, early LCQ exit would cement the "No" lean, reflecting the wisdom of crowds in this volatile qualifiers meta.

question: Will GTA 6 cost $100+?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the standard edition of Grand Theft Auto VI (GTA 6) launches at $100.00 or more. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

This market will resolve based on the pre-tax launch price in USD of the standard edition of GTA 6 on Xbox or PlayStation as listed on the Microsoft Store or PlayStation Store on the first official day of its release in the United States.

If the price differs between the Microsoft and PlayStation stores, this market will resolve according to the lower price.

This market will resolve based on the lowest price version that includes the full standard game with no DLC, etc.

If GTA 6 is not released or release is otherwise delayed beyond February 28, 2027, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be the Microsoft Store/PlayStation Store, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Take-Two Interactive's firm stance against $100 pricing for AAA games anchors the 86% market-implied odds favoring "No" on GTA 6 exceeding $100, as CEO Strauss Zelnick reaffirmed in recent earnings calls that prices will remain at the industry standard $70 amid soaring development costs. Historical precedent bolsters trader consensus—GTA 5 launched at $60 in 2013, with no confirmed leaks or official hints of a jump despite massive hype from the first trailer surpassing 200 million views. Speculative rumors on social media and forums persist but lack credible backing, while upcoming second trailer expectations focus on content, not cost, keeping sentiment firmly skeptical of a premium tag that could alienate core gamers.

question: Will Claude 5 be released by April 30, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Anthropic's Claude 5 model is made available to the general public by April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

For this market to resolve to "Yes," Claude 5 must be launched and publicly accessible, including via open beta or open rolling waitlist signups. A closed beta or any form of private access will not suffice. The release must be clearly defined and publicly announced by Anthropic as being accessible to the general public.

Claude 5 refers to a product explicitly named Claude 5 (e.g. Claude 5.0 would count), or one that is recognized as a successor to Claude 4, similar to the progression from Claude 2 to Claude 3. Products labeled as Claude 4.5 or similar will not count for this market's resolution.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Anthropic, with additional verification from a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Claude 5's release remains cautious, with implied probabilities low for an imminent launch following Anthropic's June 2024 rollout of Claude 3.5 Sonnet, which topped benchmarks against GPT-4o and Gemini 1.5 Pro. CEO Dario Amodei recently indicated Claude 4 could arrive by late 2024 or early 2025, positioning it as the next major iteration before Claude 5 potentially in mid-2025, amid no official roadmap confirmation. Competitive pressures from OpenAI's rumored GPT-5 and xAI's Grok advancements heighten urgency, but historical delays in frontier model timelines temper optimism. Watch for hints at upcoming AI summits or Anthropic's Amazon-backed updates, as slippage could push odds further out.

question: Megan Thee Stallion & Klay Thompson split in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Megan Thee Stallion and/or Klay Thompson announce their intention to legally separate or break up between market creation and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

An announcement by December 31, 2026, 11:59PM ET of their intention to separate or otherwise break up will be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution, regardless of whether the separation or breakup later actually occurs, or whether the actual separation or breakup occurs outside of the timeframe of this market.

The resolution source will be statements from Megan Thee Stallion, Klay Thompson, and/or their representatives, however a definitive consensus of credible media reporting may be considered.

question: Nothing Ever Happens: Satoshi Nakamoto
description:
This market will resolve to “Something” if any of the following conditions are met between market creation and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET:

- Satoshi moves any Bitcoin
- Epstein confirmed to be Satoshi

Otherwise, this market will resolve to “Nothing”.

1. Satoshi moves any Bitcoin

This market will resolve to “Something” if any wallet labeled as belonging to Satoshi Nakamoto on Arkham’s Intel Explorer shows an “Outflow” or “Swaps” transaction at any time during this market's above-specified time frame.

The resolution source for this market is Arkham’s Intel Explorer, specifically the entity page for Satoshi Nakamoto available at https://intel.arkm.com/explorer/entity/satoshi-nakamoto

If Arkham becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on a consensus of credible sources.

2. Epstein confirmed to be Satoshi

This market will resolve to "Something" if definitive evidence confirming that Jeffrey Epstein is Satoshi Nakamoto, the pseudonymous creator of Bitcoin, is made public during this market's above-specified time frame.

Qualifying evidence includes, but is not limited to, verified documentation, blockchain evidence, or other definitive evidence.

If Satoshi Nakamoto is conclusively identified as a group or collective rather than a single individual, definitive evidence that Jeffrey Epstein was a member of that group will qualify.

Unverified or anonymous claims, speculation, memes, jokes, sarcasm, or unsubstantiated reports will not qualify.

The primary resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

question: Will Trump and Xi not shake hands during the day of their next meeting in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the length of the longest filmed handshake between Donald Trump and Xi Jinping during the day of their next meeting in 2026 (in the local timezone of the location of their next meeting). Any handshake recorded on that date will qualify.

If no handshake occurs during the date of their next meeting, or if no meeting takes place by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No Handshake”.

If a handshake is photographed but not captured on video, this market will resolve to "Photographed only".

Duration will be measured from the exact moment their hands make initial physical contact until the exact moment either breaks contact.

If the measured duration falls exactly on the boundary between two duration brackets, this market will resolve to the higher bracket.

Qualifying Requirements:
The handshake must be voluntary, intentional, and in person.
Direct hand-to-hand contact is required (gloves or mittens are permitted).
The handshake must be clearly visible on video from start to finish.

Non-qualifying examples:
Fist bumps, hugs, waves, or other non-handshake greetings.
Any handshake that is too unclear to measure.
Only photographic evidence exists without a way to measure duration.

The resolution source will be video footage.

events.title: How long will Trump and Xi shake hands when they meet?
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the length of the longest filmed handshake between Donald Trump and Xi Jinping during the day of their next meeting in 2026 (in the local timezone of the location of their next meeting). Any handshake recorded on that date will qualify.

If no handshake occurs during the date of their next meeting, or if no meeting takes place by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No Handshake”.

If a handshake is photographed but not captured on video, this market will resolve to "Photographed only".

Duration will be measured from the exact moment their hands make initial physical contact until the exact moment either breaks contact.

If the measured duration falls exactly on the boundary between two duration brackets, this market will resolve to the higher bracket.

Qualifying Requirements:
The handshake must be voluntary, intentional, and in person.
Direct hand-to-hand contact is required (gloves or mittens are permitted).
The handshake must be clearly visible on video from start to finish.

Non-qualifying examples:
Fist bumps, hugs, waves, or other non-handshake greetings.
Any handshake that is too unclear to measure.
Only photographic evidence exists without a way to measure duration.

The resolution source will be video footage.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors a Trump-Xi handshake lasting 10 seconds or longer, with 15s+ at 36% edging 10–15s at 31%, reflecting the president-elect's history of extended, firm grips—seen in past summits like 2017's Mar-a-Lago meeting—against Xi's more reserved diplomatic style. Recent post-election phone discussions signal potential for an early bilateral summit, keeping longer durations viable amid choreographed protocol norms that typically cap handshakes at 6–10s. The tight race stems from uncertainty over meeting timing and optics, with low no-handshake odds (6.7%) due to tradition. Confirmed summit details or advance team previews could boost longer buckets, while frosty tariff rhetoric might shorten sentiment.

question: Megan Thee Stallion and Klay Thompson engaged in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if it is announced that Megan Thee Stallion is engaged to be married to Klay Thompson by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If it is announced that Megan Thee Stallion and Klay Thompson have married, it will qualify for a "Yes" resolution.  

The primary resolution source for this market will be information from Megan Thee Stallion and/or Klay Thompson or one of their representatives, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.title: Megan Thee Stallion and Klay Thompson engaged in 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader skepticism dominates the Megan Thee Stallion and Klay Thompson engagement market, with "No" holding a 55% implied probability, driven primarily by the absence of any confirmed romantic link beyond a single viral courtside moment at a November Dallas Mavericks game. During the matchup against the Knicks, Thompson's playful heart gesture toward the rapper sparked fleeting social media buzz and tabloid speculation, but both parties have issued no statements confirming even casual dating—Megan recently emphasized her single status in interviews, while Klay's post-injury focus remains on NBA playoffs. Historical precedent in celebrity sports crossovers shows such flirtations rarely evolve into engagements within a year, let alone by 2026, tempering trader optimism absent further developments like joint appearances or public affection. Key watch: holiday events or All-Star weekend sightings could shift odds.

question: Nothing Ever Happens: Obama
description:
This market will resolve to “Something” if any of the following conditions are met between market creation and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET:

- Obama federally charged
- Obama arrested
- Obama divorce

Otherwise, this market will resolve to “Nothing”.

The full rules for this market can be found here: 
https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/NEH_Obama.pdf

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on the "Nothing" outcome at 82% for Obama reflects his sustained low public profile in the 2024 election cycle, with no major speeches, campaign appearances, or policy interventions despite Democratic shifts like Biden's withdrawal and Harris's nomination. Obama's July 26 endorsement of Harris via a personal Medium post was subdued and pre-dated the DNC, where he notably did not speak, aligning with his post-presidency pattern of selective involvement focused on his foundation and memoirs. A mild COVID-19 diagnosis in late July drew brief attention but carried no political ramifications. Absent scheduled events or catalysts, traders price minimal near-term disruption from the former president.

question: Services Down Parlay
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if all of the following conditions are met between market creation and March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET:

- AWS service disrupted
- Critical Discord Incident
- Critical Cloudflare incident

Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will remain open until it is confirmed that at least one of the above conditions has not been met, or until all of the above conditions have been met within the specified timeframe.

The full rules for this market can be found here: 
https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/Services+Down+Parlay.pdf

events.title: Services Down Parlay

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on the "Services Down Parlay" market heavily favors "No" at 84.3% implied probability, driven by the exceptional uptime records of major cloud providers like AWS, Azure, GCP, and Cloudflare, whose redundant architectures make simultaneous outages vanishingly rare—historical data shows zero such events in over five years. Recent status page checks confirm all services green with no disruptions reported in the past 24 hours, quelling outage rumors amplified on social media. Key upcoming catalysts include AWS re:Invent echoes and routine maintenance windows, but absent correlated cyber threats or geomagnetic storms, traders see minimal risk of the parlay resolving "Yes," aligning odds with industry SLAs exceeding 99.99%.

question: Will CDU win the most seats in the 2026 Sachsen-Anhalt parliamentary elections?
description:
Parliamentary elections to elect the Landtag of Sachsen-Anhalt are scheduled to take place on September 6, 2026.

This market will resolve to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the state parliament of Sachsen-Anhalt (Landtag) as a result of this election.

If voting in the Sachsen-Anhalt election for the Landtag does not occur by January 31, 2027, this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party that received a greater number of valid votes. In the event that results in a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the party whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party or coalition.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as published on the official website of the Landtag of Sachsen-Anhalt (https://www.landtag.sachsen-anhalt.de/).

events.title: Sachsen-Anhalt Parliamentary Election Winner
events.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: AfD's commanding 91.5% implied probability on Polymarket for topping the Sachsen-Anhalt Landtagswahl stems from consistent poll leads of 32-36% in recent INSA and Forsa surveys, outpacing CDU at 26-29% and rising BSW at 14-18%, amid persistent eastern German discontent over migration and economic stagnation favoring the populist right. Traders' skin-in-the-game consensus reflects AfD's structural edge in the state since 2021, reinforced by federal trends post-September state votes in Saxony and Thuringia. Realistic challenges include a late CDU surge via coalition signaling, BSW momentum from Wagenknecht's anti-establishment appeal, or polling overstatement as in past East German races, though the election remains distant in 2026 with no imminent catalysts.

question: Will The Greens win the most seats in the 2026 Sachsen-Anhalt parliamentary elections?
description:
Parliamentary elections to elect the Landtag of Sachsen-Anhalt are scheduled to take place on September 6, 2026.

This market will resolve to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the state parliament of Sachsen-Anhalt (Landtag) as a result of this election.

If voting in the Sachsen-Anhalt election for the Landtag does not occur by January 31, 2027, this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party that received a greater number of valid votes. In the event that results in a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the party whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party or coalition.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as published on the official website of the Landtag of Sachsen-Anhalt (https://www.landtag.sachsen-anhalt.de/).

question: Will CDU win the second most seats in the 2026 Rhineland-Palatinate parliamentary elections?
description:
Parliamentary elections to elect the Landtag of Rhineland-Palatinate (Rheinland-Pfalz) are scheduled to take place on March 22, 2026.

This market will resolve to the political party that wins the second-greatest number of seats in the formal deliberative assembly of Rhineland-Palatinate (Landtag) as a result of this election.

If voting in the Rhineland-Palatinate election for the Landtag does not occur by July 31, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The named parties or coalitions will be primarily ranked by the number of seats won in the specified election. If two or more parties are tied on seats, ties will be broken by the total number of valid votes received, with higher vote totals ranking higher. If parties remain tied, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the listed party abbreviations. This market will resolve to the party that occupies the second-highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

This market will resolve based on the election results, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Election Office of Rhineland-Palatinate (Landeswahlleiter Rheinland-Pfalz https://www.wahlen.rlp.de/landtagswahl/ergebnisse)

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent polls for the Rheinland-Palatinate Landtag election in March 2026 show the CDU leading with 32-36% support, placing the SPD in a firm second at 24-28%, well ahead of the Grüne (12-15%), AfD (9-12%), and others below 8%. This trader consensus reflects the SPD's stable position as junior coalition partner under incumbent Minister-President Malu Dreyer, bolstered by consistent survey data from INSA and Forschungsgruppe Wahlen amid national CDU momentum. No party has closed the gap to challenge second place, with Greens declining and AfD stagnant locally. Upsets could arise from CDU scandals eroding its lead or a late AfD surge, though current evidence suggests low probability.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent polls for the Rheinland-Palatinate Landtag election in March 2026 show the CDU leading with 32-36% support, placing the SPD in a firm second at 24-28%, well ahead of the Grüne (12-15%), AfD (9-12%), and others below 8%. This trader consensus reflects the SPD's stable position as junior coalition partner under incumbent Minister-President Malu Dreyer, bolstered by consistent survey data from INSA and Forschungsgruppe Wahlen amid national CDU momentum. No party has closed the gap to challenge second place, with Greens declining and AfD stagnant locally. Upsets could arise from CDU scandals eroding its lead or a late AfD surge, though current evidence suggests low probability.

question: Will CDU win the most seats in the 2026 Mecklenburg-Vorpommern parliamentary elections?
description:
Parliamentary elections to elect the Landtag of Mecklenburg-Vorpommern are scheduled to take place on September 20, 2026.

This market will resolve to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the state parliament of Mecklenburg-Vorpommern (Landtag) as a result of this election.

If voting in the Mecklenburg-Vorpommern election for the Landtag does not occur by January 31, 2027, this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party that received a greater number of valid votes. In the event that results in a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the party whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as published on the official Mecklenburg-Vorpommern elections page of the Landesamt für innere Verwaltung (www.laiv-mv.de/Wahlen/Landtagswahlen/2026/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus overwhelmingly prices Alternative for Germany (AfD) as the winner of the Mecklenburg-Vorpommern Landtag election on September 29, driven by consistent poll leads of 30-33% from INSA and Forschungsgruppe Wahlen surveys through mid-September. Regional frustrations over migration policy, economic stagnation, and infrastructure in eastern Germany bolster AfD's dominance, while incumbent SPD trails at 22-25% amid national coalition instability. Recent catalysts include AfD's strong showing in the September 15 TV debate and steady voter migration from CDU, keeping challengers like BSW under 8%. Markets reflect low upset probability, though final turnout and weather could influence results.

Recent catalysts include AfD's strong showing in the September 15 TV debate and steady voter migration from CDU, keeping challengers like BSW under 8%. Markets reflect low upset probability, though final turnout and weather could influence results.

question: Love Wins: 2026 Edition
description:
This market will resolve to “No” if any of the following conditions are met between market creation and December 31, 2026, 11:59PM ET:

- Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce do not get married
- Tom Holland and Zendaya do not get married
- Timothée Chalamet and Kylie Jenner do not get engaged

Otherwise, this market will resolve to “Yes”.

If any mentioned couple announces that they will end their romantic relationship within the stated timeframe, this market will resolve to “No”.

The full rules for this market can be found here: 
https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/Love+Wins_+2026+edition+(1).pdf

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus tilts slightly against overturning Obergefell v. Hodges before 2027, with "No" at 53%, driven by strong public support for same-sex marriage (around 70% in recent Gallup polls) and President-elect Trump's public endorsement of gay marriage rights, tempering fears of aggressive federal rollback. The balance stems from the Supreme Court's 6-3 conservative majority—emboldened post-Dobbs—yet adherence to stare decisis and lack of a direct challenge case, despite state-level restrictions in places like Tennessee bubbling up. Key tipping points include 2025 state lawsuits escalating to SCOTUS, justice vacancies enabling Trump appointees, or 2026 midterm shifts influencing litigation momentum, all under scrutiny as cultural flashpoints.

question: Will the Democratic Party of Korea win 0 or 1 seats in South Korea’s June 3, 2026 parliamentary by-elections?
description:
At least four South Korean parliamentary seats will be contested in by-elections on June 3, 2026, held alongside the nationwide local elections taking place on the same day. Re-elections are equivalent to by-elections for the purposes of this market.

This market will resolve according to the number of National Assembly seats won by the Democratic Party of Korea (DP) in parliamentary by-elections scheduled for June 3, 2026.

Any seat won by the Democratic Party of Korea in these elections will count, regardless of the party that controlled the relevant seat prior to these elections. Seats uncontested during the June 3 election will not have any impact on the resolution of this market.

If the results of these elections are not known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to the lowest range bracket.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the specified party in these elections, not any coalition or alliance of which it may be a part.

This market will resolve based on the results of the elections as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the South Korean government, specifically the National Election Commission (http://nec.go.kr/site/nec/main.do).

Note: this market includes any National Assembly seats contested in by-elections scheduled for June 3, 2026, not just elections which are scheduled as of this market’s creation.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a 61.5% implied probability for South Korea's Democratic Party (DP) winning 10 or more seats in upcoming National Assembly by-elections across 12 contested districts, driven by recent polls showing DP leads of 10-20 points in most races amid ruling People Power Party (PPP) turmoil following President Yoon Suk Yeol's short-lived martial law declaration on December 3, 2024. This scandal has eroded PPP support to historic lows below 30%, favoring DP in urban strongholds. Secondary odds for 8-9 seats (39.1%) account for potential upsets in rural swing districts, while lower outcomes reflect DP's strong base rates in prior by-elections. Voter turnout and final campaigning could shift dynamics before the vote.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket favors the People Power Party (PPP) capturing exactly 3 seats (55.5%) in upcoming South Korean National Assembly by-elections for 6 contested districts, driven by recent district-level polls showing PPP competitive in three conservative-leaning areas while trailing in Democratic Party strongholds. Low presidential approval ratings around 30% for Yoon Suk Yeol have pressured PPP candidates amid scandals like the marine reservist death controversy, yet incumbency advantages and targeted campaigning have bolstered prospects in Gyeonggi and Busan races. Final pre-election surveys released this week indicate a split outcome, with no path to sweeping wins or total losses, as traders weigh base rates from PPP's partial successes in prior 2024 by-elections. Markets await voting on November 13.

At least four South Korean parliamentary seats will be contested in by-elections on June 3, 2026, held alongside the nationwide local elections taking place on the same day. Re-elections are equivalent to by-elections for the purposes of this market.

This market will resolve according to the number of National Assembly seats won by the People Power Party (PP) in parliamentary by-elections scheduled for June 3, 2026.

Any seat won by the People Power Party in these elections will count, regardless of the party that controlled the relevant seat prior to these elections. Seats uncontested during the June 3 election will not have any impact on the resolution of this market.

If the results of these elections are not known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to the lowest range bracket.
This market’s resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the specified party in these elections, not any coalition or alliance of which it may be a part.

This market will resolve based on the results of the elections as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the South Korean government, specifically the National Election Commission.

Note: this market includes any National Assembly seats contested in by-elections scheduled for June 3, 2026, not just elections which are scheduled as of this market’s creation.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket favors the People Power Party (PPP) capturing exactly 3 seats (55.5%) in upcoming South Korean National Assembly by-elections for 6 contested districts, driven by recent district-level polls showing PPP competitive in three conservative-leaning areas while trailing in Democratic Party strongholds. Low presidential approval ratings around 30% for Yoon Suk Yeol have pressured PPP candidates amid scandals like the marine reservist death controversy, yet incumbency advantages and targeted campaigning have bolstered prospects in Gyeonggi and Busan races. Final pre-election surveys released this week indicate a split outcome, with no path to sweeping wins or total losses, as traders weigh base rates from PPP's partial successes in prior 2024 by-elections. Markets await voting on November 13.

question: Will the People Power Party (PPP) win the most seats in South Korea’s 2026 parliamentary by-elections?
description:
At least four South Korean parliamentary seats will be contested in by-elections on June 3, 2026, held alongside the nationwide local elections taking place on the same day. Re-elections are equivalent to by-elections for the purposes of this market.

This market will resolve to the party that wins the greatest number of National Assembly seats in the parliamentary by-elections scheduled for June 3, 2026.

Any seat won in these elections will count, regardless of the party that controlled the relevant seat prior to these elections. Seats uncontested during the June 3 election will not have any impact on the resolution of this market.

If the results of these elections are not known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.
This market’s resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the specified parties in these elections, not any coalition or alliance of which they may be a part.

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party that received a greater number of valid votes across all the relevant parliamentary by-elections. In the event that results in a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the party whose listed full name appears first in alphabetical order.

This market will resolve based on the results of the elections as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the South Korean government, specifically the National Election Commission.

Note: this market includes any National Assembly seats contested in by-elections scheduled for June 3, 2026, not just elections which are scheduled as of this market’s creation.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors the Democratic Party of Korea (DP) at 94.2% implied probability to win the South Korea by-elections, driven by ruling People Power Party (PPP) unpopularity amid President Yoon Suk Yeol's sub-30% approval ratings, economic pressures, and ongoing scandals like the first lady investigation. Recent polling shows DP candidates leading by wide margins in key districts, bolstered by opposition unity and voter turnout favoring change. Rebuilding Korea Party trails at 17.1% on leader Lee Jun-seok's momentum but lacks broad appeal. Realistic challenges include a PPP surge from conservative mobilization, DP candidate controversy, or unexpectedly high youth turnout boosting smaller parties like the Progressive Party. Markets await final candidate announcements and early voting data.

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party that received a greater number of valid votes across all the relevant parliamentary by-elections. In the event that results in a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the party whose listed full name appears first in alphabetical order.

This market will resolve based on the results of the elections as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the South Korean government, specifically the National Election Commission.

Note: this market includes any National Assembly seats contested in by-elections scheduled for June 3, 2026, not just elections which are scheduled as of this market’s creation.

events.title: South Korea By-Elections: Party Winner
events.description:
At least four South Korean parliamentary seats will be contested in by-elections on June 3, 2026, held alongside the nationwide local elections taking place on the same day. Re-elections are equivalent to by-elections for the purposes of this market.

This market will resolve to the party that wins the greatest number of National Assembly seats in the parliamentary by-elections scheduled for June 3, 2026.

Any seat won in these elections will count, regardless of the party that controlled the relevant seat prior to these elections. Seats uncontested during the June 3 election will not have any impact on the resolution of this market.

If the results of these elections are not known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.
This market’s resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the specified parties in these elections, not any coalition or alliance of which they may be a part.

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party that received a greater number of valid votes across all the relevant parliamentary by-elections. In the event that results in a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the party whose listed full name appears first in alphabetical order.

This market will resolve based on the results of the elections as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the South Korean government, specifically the National Election Commission.

Note: this market includes any National Assembly seats contested in by-elections scheduled for June 3, 2026, not just elections which are scheduled as of this market’s creation.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors the Democratic Party of Korea (DP) at 94.2% implied probability to win the South Korea by-elections, driven by ruling People Power Party (PPP) unpopularity amid President Yoon Suk Yeol's sub-30% approval ratings, economic pressures, and ongoing scandals like the first lady investigation. Recent polling shows DP candidates leading by wide margins in key districts, bolstered by opposition unity and voter turnout favoring change. Rebuilding Korea Party trails at 17.1% on leader Lee Jun-seok's momentum but lacks broad appeal. Realistic challenges include a PPP surge from conservative mobilization, DP candidate controversy, or unexpectedly high youth turnout boosting smaller parties like the Progressive Party. Markets await final candidate announcements and early voting data.

question: Will Tarcisio de Freitas finish in second place in the first round of the 2026 Brazilian presidential election?
description:
A presidential election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate who receives the second-most valid votes in the first round of this election.

The named candidates will be primarily ranked by the number of valid votes received in the specified election. If two or more candidates are tied on valid votes, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the candidates' last names. This market will resolve to the candidate that occupies the second-highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

If the result of this election isn't known definitively by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., https://dadosabertos.tse.jus.br/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent national polls from Quaest, Paraná Pesquisas, and Datafolha position Tarcísio de Freitas as the clear first-round leader with 35-44% support in Brazil's 2026 presidential election, elevating Flávio Bolsonaro to second at 13-16% as traders consolidate bets on him at 63.5% odds for that spot. Jair Bolsonaro's endorsement and family brand have boosted Flávio amid right-wing unity, while President Lula's support has slipped to 7-11% amid succession uncertainty within the PT party. Ratinho Júnior trails at 9-12% despite gubernatorial strength, with Fernando Haddad and others lower due to limited national traction. Upcoming polls and candidate filings could shift these first-round dynamics.

question: Will Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva win the first round of the 2026 Brazilian presidential election by less than 5%?
description:
A presidential election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the margin of victory between the top two candidates in the first round of the next Brazil Presidential Election.

For the purpose of this market, the “margin of victory” is defined as the absolute difference between the percentages of valid votes received by the first- and second-place candidates. Percentages of the valid votes received by each candidate will be determined by dividing the total number of valid votes each of the top two candidates receives by the sum of all valid votes cast in the election.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

This market will resolve based on the official vote count once the vote count has been made official.

If a recount is initiated before the vote total has been made official, the market will remain open until the recount is completed and the vote is made official.

If the result of this election isn't known definitively by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., https://dadosabertos.tse.jus.br/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors a narrow Lula da Silva win in Brazil's 2026 presidential election first round, pricing his margin under 5% at 36% and 5-10% at 18%, reflecting recent polls from Datafolha and Quaest where Lula leads with 35-42% against fragmented right-wing challengers like Tarcísio de Freitas (25-30%) and Ratinho Júnior (12-18%). Flávio Bolsonaro's under-5% margin at 17% captures bets on Bolsonaro-family consolidation amid Jair Bolsonaro's ineligibility, though he trails in surveys. Lula's incumbency edge persists despite dipping approval ratings tied to inflation and fiscal austerity, while no candidate polls near a first-round majority; upcoming primary filings and regional gubernatorial results could shift odds.

For the purpose of this market, the “margin of victory” is defined as the absolute difference between the percentages of valid votes received by the first- and second-place candidates. Percentages of the valid votes received by each candidate will be determined by dividing the total number of valid votes each of the top two candidates receives by the sum of all valid votes cast in the election.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

This market will resolve based on the official vote count once the vote count has been made official.

If a recount is initiated before the vote total has been made official, the market will remain open until the recount is completed and the vote is made official.

If the result of this election isn't known definitively by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., https://dadosabertos.tse.jus.br/).

question: Vanderbilt Commodores vs. Missouri Tigers
description:
In the upcoming CBB game, scheduled for February 17 at 12:00 AM ET:

If the Vanderbilt Commodores win, the market will resolve to "Vanderbilt Commodores".

If the Missouri Tigers win, the market will resolve to "Missouri Tigers".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.

If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

events.title: Vanderbilt Commodores vs. Missouri Tigers

eventMetadata.context_description: Missouri Tigers' 54.5% implied probability as slight favorites stems from their top-25 ranking, explosive offense averaging 37 points per game, and home advantage at Faurot Field against a scrappy Vanderbilt squad. Recent developments like Missouri's bye-week rest versus Vandy's road wear tilt sentiment narrowly, but traders eye injury updates—Missouri DE Gabe Williams questionable, Vandy RB Nate Williams banged up—as catalysts that could flip odds, with crowd wisdom reflecting historical SEC volatility where underdogs cover 45% on the road.

question: Clavicular charged again by June 30?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any Federal or State jurisdiction of the United States formally charges or otherwise announces a criminal indictment of Braden Eric Peters (known as Clavicular) between market creation and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

For the purposes of this market the District of Columbia and any county, municipality, or other subdivision of a State shall be included within the definition of a State.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from US governmental sources, however a wide consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

Court records show his initial case remains in pretrial stages with no escalations, and the streaming community's real-time chatter on X reflects fading buzz without fresh drama, aligning with historical patterns where isolated streamer scraps rarely lead to repeat filings absent viral escalations. Key catalysts ahead include potential June hearings or social media blowups, but current quiet in legal updates and public sentiment underpins the market's skepticism for additional charges before the deadline.

question: Will Partido Liberal (PL) win the most seats in the next Brazilian Senate election?
description:
The next federal Senate election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026, in which two-thirds of the Senate’s 81 seats will be contested.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the next Brazilian Senate election. 

Only seats contested in the next Brazilian Senate election will be considered.

If the result of this election isn't known definitively by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., www.tse.jus.br/eleicoes/resultados-eleicoes).

events.title: Next Brazil Senate Election: Most Seats Won
events.description:
The next federal Senate election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026, in which two-thirds of the Senate’s 81 seats will be contested.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the next Brazilian Senate election. 

Only seats contested in the next Brazilian Senate election will be considered.

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the Brazilian Senate, not by any federation or coalition it may be a part of.

If the result of this election isn't known definitively by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., www.tse.jus.br/eleicoes/resultados-eleicoes).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus strongly favors the PL party to secure the most seats in Brazil's 2026 Senate election, with 77% implied probability, driven by its status as the largest bloc in the lower house and strong performance in the October 2024 municipal elections, where it captured numerous mayoral wins amid center-right momentum. MDB trails at 7.5%, bolstered by incumbents defending seats in key states like Mato Grosso and Maranhão, while PDT, PP, PODEMOS, and REPUBLICANOS hover around 4-5% on regional strongholds and coalition potential. Recent Datafolha polls reinforce PL's lead in Senate race projections, though fluid alliances under President Lula's administration and economic shifts could alter dynamics before candidate filings in 2026.

question: Will Partido Liberal (PL) hold the most seats as a result of the next Brazilian Senate election?
description:
The next federal Senate election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026, in which two-thirds of the Senate’s 81 seats will be contested.

This market will resolve according to the political party that holds the greatest number of seats in the next Brazilian Senate as a result of the next Brazilian Senate election. 

All seats, not only the ones contested in the next Brazilian Senate election, will be considered.

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats held, this market will resolve in favor of the party whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats held by the named party in the Brazilian Senate as a result of the next Brazilian Senate election, not by any federation or coalition it may be a part of.

If the result of this election isn't known definitively by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., www.tse.jus.br/eleicoes/resultados-eleicoes) or the Brazilian Senate (https://www25.senado.leg.br/web/senadores/em-exercicio/-/e/por-partido).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket strongly favors the PL party to hold the most seats in Brazil's Senate after the 2026 election, with implied odds at 66.5%, driven by its current leading position of 14 seats from the 2022 cycle and incumbency advantages in the 27 seats up for renewal. PL's momentum stems from capturing 12 of those 2022 seats amid a right-wing surge and replicating success in October 2024 municipal elections, where it won the most mayoral races nationwide. Challengers like PDT (11.1%) benefit from Northeast strongholds, while PSB, MDB, and UNIÃO trail due to fragmented center-left alliances under President Lula and centrist volatility. Upcoming polls and candidate announcements could shift dynamics, as base rates show incumbents often retain edges in staggered Senate races.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats held by the named party in the Brazilian Senate as a result of the next Brazilian Senate election, not by any federation or coalition it may be a part of.

If the result of this election isn't known definitively by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket strongly favors the PL party to hold the most seats in Brazil's Senate after the 2026 election, with implied odds at 66.5%, driven by its current leading position of 14 seats from the 2022 cycle and incumbency advantages in the 27 seats up for renewal. PL's momentum stems from capturing 12 of those 2022 seats amid a right-wing surge and replicating success in October 2024 municipal elections, where it won the most mayoral races nationwide. Challengers like PDT (11.1%) benefit from Northeast strongholds, while PSB, MDB, and UNIÃO trail due to fragmented center-left alliances under President Lula and centrist volatility. Upcoming polls and candidate announcements could shift dynamics, as base rates show incumbents often retain edges in staggered Senate races.

question: U.S. anti-cartel ground operation in Mexico by June 30?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if U.S. government personnel (military, DEA, CIA, or any other agency) directly participates on the ground in an anti-cartel operation on Mexican soil by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

U.S. personnel must directly participate to qualify. U.S. personnel involved in intelligence, surveillance, logistical, support, or advisory roles will not count. 

Only direct U.S. participation which is either confirmed by the U.S. Government or an overwhelming consensus of reporting will count. For example, previous operations such as the 2014 capture of Joaquín "El Chapo" Guzmán, in which U.S. forces were rumored to have been embedded with Mexican Marines, would not qualify. 

The primary resolution source for this market will be official statements from the U.S. government, however an overwhelming consensus of reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects low implied probability for a U.S. anti-cartel ground operation in Mexico, driven by Mexico's staunch defense of sovereignty and lack of official U.S. military planning. President-elect Trump's December 2024 designation of major cartels as foreign terrorist organizations, coupled with threats of force over fentanyl flows and border security, prompted President Sheinbaum's firm rejection of any "invasion." No confirmed troop deployments, congressional authorizations, or joint operations exist, prioritizing diplomatic pressure and USMCA trade leverage. Trump's January 20, 2025, inauguration and early bilateral summits represent key upcoming catalysts that could escalate or de-escalate tensions.

question: Greta Thunberg arrested by June 30?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Greta Thunberg is arrested or detained by law enforcement between market creation and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Temporary holding at a detention facility while awaiting a judge’s decision on whether to grant a detention warrant qualifies, so long as they are held by law enforcement in an official capacity during that time.

A qualifying arrest/detention includes:
* Being taken into physical custody by law enforcement authorities (including federal, state, local, military, or international law enforcement)
* Voluntarily surrendering to law enforcement authorities in response to an arrest warrant
* Being formally booked or processed following detention
* Being handcuffed by a state authority and taken to a police station
* Surrendering at a police station or courthouse with their attorney
* Being placed under house arrest or electronic monitoring

The following scenarios will NOT qualify as an arrest/detention
* An arrest warrant being issued but not executed
* Being questioned or interviewed without arrest
* Being named in an indictment without arrest

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the arresting government and law enforcement; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Greta Thunberg's repeated participation in civil disobedience protests, including recent pro-Palestine demonstrations in Europe, drives the 57.5% implied probability for her arrest by June 30, reflecting trader consensus on elevated risk from police interventions during disruptive actions. She has faced detention multiple times this year alone, such as in the Netherlands in April for blocking a highway during a climate protest and in Malmö amid Eurovision tensions in May, where activists clashed with authorities. With summer activism ramping up—including potential road blockades or occupations—no confirmed plans exist, but her pattern and ongoing solidarity campaigns with Gaza flotilla efforts sustain market pricing amid uncertainty over law enforcement responses.

description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of StandX's token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If StandX (https://x.com/StandX_Official) doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

question: Pam Bondi out as Attorney General by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Pam Bondi ceases to be the US Attorney General for any period of time between market creation and March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Bondi's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the Trump administration, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Pam Bondi out as Attorney General by March 31?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Pam Bondi ceases to be the US Attorney General for any period of time between market creation and March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Bondi's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the Trump administration, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors Pam Bondi remaining Attorney General through March 31, driven by Republican Senate control (53-47 majority) and her nomination's smooth progress following the January 15 Judiciary Committee hearing and subsequent advancement on a party-line vote. No major scandals or opposition have emerged since her November nomination, unlike predecessor Matt Gaetz's withdrawal, positioning her for likely full Senate confirmation before inauguration-related deadlines. This reflects bettors' assessment of low removal risk absent unforeseen events like a disqualifying revelation or bipartisan blockade, with Trump's transition momentum further bolstering stability in the Justice Department leadership slot.

question: Will UK GDP growth in Q1 2026 be negative?
description:
This market will resolve according to the UK real gross domestic product (GDP) growth rate (% change) for Q1 of 2026 compared with the same quarter a year ago in the "GDP First quarterly estimate, UK" release for Q1 of 2026, scheduled for May 14, 2026.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

The GDP release will be made available here: https://www.ons.gov.uk/economy/grossdomesticproductgdp/bulletins/gdpfirstquarterlyestimateuk/previousreleases

If no data for the specified quarter is released by the date the next quarter's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available quarter.

Note: data from the initial release of the referenced GDP report is what will be used to resolve this market. Data may be revised during the following quarter or as a part of the next estimate's publication, however any revisions to GDP report data made after the initial release will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors subdued UK GDP growth in Q1 2026, with a 50.5% implied probability for 0.0-0.3% quarterly expansion, reflecting economist forecasts from the Bank of England and Office for Budget Responsibility projecting annual growth near 1.7% amid persistent headwinds. Primary drivers include the BoE's restrictive 5% Bank Rate curbing consumer spending and investment, alongside Chancellor Reeves' October budget hikes to employer National Insurance that dented business confidence, as evidenced by PMI surveys dipping below expansion. Recent Q3 2024 GDP rose just 0.1% quarter-on-quarter per ONS data, underscoring weak momentum, while upcoming December MPC meeting odds imply gradual rate cuts insufficient for acceleration, positioning sub-0.6% outcomes as leading bets at nearly 80% market-implied odds.

question: Howard Lutnick out as Secretary of Commerce by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Howard Lutnick ceases to be the US Secretary of Commerce for any period of time between market creation and March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Lutnick's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the Trump administration however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Howard Lutnick out as Secretary of Commerce by March 31?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Howard Lutnick ceases to be the US Secretary of Commerce for any period of time between market creation and March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Lutnick's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the Trump administration however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trump's nomination of Howard Lutnick for Secretary of Commerce advanced swiftly through Senate confirmation, with his January 29 hearing before the Commerce Committee featuring minimal partisan friction despite Democratic queries on tariffs and cryptocurrency ties. The Republican Senate majority (53-47) enabled a February 18 voice vote approval without formal opposition, followed by Lutnick's swearing-in on February 20. Trader consensus at 98.3% "No" reflects this stability, bolstered by Lutnick's donor background and lack of scandals, contrasting smoother Commerce confirmations historically. Realistic shifts could arise from unforeseen policy disputes with Trump, personal health issues, or emerging ethics probes, though evidence points to continuity through March 31.

question: Will Mauricio Pochettino be appointed as manager of Tottenham?
description:
This market will resolve to the person who is appointed as the next permanent manager of Tottenham Hotspur.

If no permanent manager is appointed by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other". 

Appointments of 'interim,' 'caretaker,' or other non-permanent managers will not impact this market's resolution.

An announcement of a new permanent manager's appointment before this market's close date will immediately resolve this market to the corresponding option, regardless of when the announced appointment goes into effect.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Tottenham Hotspur; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.title: Next Tottenham manager?
events.description:
This market will resolve to the person who is appointed as the next permanent manager of Tottenham Hotspur.

If no permanent manager is appointed by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other". 

Appointments of 'interim,' 'caretaker,' or other non-permanent managers will not impact this market's resolution.

An announcement of a new permanent manager's appointment before this market's close date will immediately resolve this market to the corresponding option, regardless of when the announced appointment goes into effect.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Tottenham Hotspur; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment for Tottenham's next manager hinges on mounting pressure on Ange Postecoglou amid a dismal run, including back-to-back Premier League defeats to Chelsea and Manchester City, leaving Spurs 14th after 15 matches and prompting sack speculation from club sources. Mauricio Pochettino leads at 35% implied probability due to his successful prior Spurs tenure (2014-19), deep club knowledge, and availability post-Chelsea; recent amicable Chelsea exit bolsters his case. Robert De Zerbi (24.5%) gains from his progressive Brighton spell and tactical fit, while Edin Terzic (20.4%) appeals for Dortmund success and youth. Ryan Mason (18.8%) reflects internal continuity as ex-interim boss, with odds capturing Levy's preference for proven Premier League operators amid relegation fears.

question: Will any AI model reach a Chatbot Arena score of at least 1650 by December 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if any model on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) reaches at least the specified Arena Score by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Results from the 'Score' section on the 'Text Arena' Leaderboard tab (https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text), with the style control unchecked, will be used to resolve this market.

The resolution source is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/). If this source is temporarily unavailable, the market remains open until it is accessible again; if permanently unavailable, this market will resolve to "No".

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Chatbot Arena's top Elo score by December 31 hinges on the rapid iteration of frontier AI models, with current leader Gemini 2.0 Flash-Exp (Thinking mode) at 1332 Elo as of mid-December, up from 1280 for Claude 3.5 Sonnet earlier this year. Google's December 11 Gemini 2.0 launch, featuring enhanced reasoning chains, has catalyzed optimism for further gains, while OpenAI's o1 series and Anthropic's teased updates intensify competition on LMSYS's blind-voting leaderboard. Traders eye end-of-year releases like xAI's Grok-3 for potential 1350+ breakthroughs, though historical slips in AI timelines temper aggressive bets; resolution ties to verified peaks on lmsys.org without post-hoc adjustments.

question: Will OpenAI be the first company to have an AI model hit 1550 on Chatbot Arena in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the listed entity, which is the first to reach an Arena Score of 1550+ on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. 

Results from the "Text Arena" section on the leaderboard/text tab of https://lmarena.ai/ with the style control unchecked (https://arena.ai/leaderboard/text/overall-no-style-control) will be used to resolve this market.

If no company's model reaches 1550+ Arena Score by the specified time, this market will resolve to "None in 2026".

If the first model to reach a 1550+ Arena Score belongs to any company other than the listed ones, this market will be resolved to "Other".

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. eventMetadata.context_description: Trader skepticism dominates the Chatbot Arena 1550 Elo market for 2026, with "None" at 45% implied probability, as current leaders like Google's Gemini 2.0 Flash Experimental (1378 Elo) and Anthropic's Claude 3.5 Sonnet Update (1373) remain over 170 points short, amid slowing benchmark gains from data scarcity and compute limits. Anthropic leads contenders at 27% on Claude 4 training rumors signaling major scaling, bolstered by Sonnet's recent Arena surge. Google (13.5%) gains from Gemini 2.0 multimodal advances, while OpenAI (7%) lags despite o1's reasoning prowess due to middling Elo. Rising Chinese models like DeepSeek (2.1%) and Alibaba's Qwen reflect open-source momentum, but traders eye 2026 releases like Grok-3 and Mistral Large 2 as key tests against scaling plateaus.

question: Iran agrees to end enrichment of uranium by June 30?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Iran publicly agrees to end all enrichment of uranium by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An official pledge by Iran to end all enrichment of Uranium will qualify for a “Yes” resolution, whether as a unilateral announcement or part of an agreement with the U.S. or Israel.

Any agreement or pledge made before the resolution date of this market will qualify, regardless of if/when the agreement goes into effect.

An agreement by Iran to end all enrichment of uranium for any amount of time will count.

An agreement by Iran to end all enrichment of uranium as a precondition of a more comprehensive peace process or deal will qualify, even if the agreement is not finalized or part of a formalized peace deal. 

Agreements to merely limit or cap the level or quality of enrichment—such as reducing enrichment to below weapons-grade thresholds—will not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Iran's Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei and top negotiators have repeatedly affirmed the country's right to uranium enrichment, rejecting any freeze or halt as a nonstarter in ongoing indirect talks with the US mediated by Oman. This stance, reiterated in recent weeks amid stalled sixth-round discussions, underpins trader consensus at 79% odds against an agreement by June 30. The IAEA's June 12 board resolution censuring Iran for non-cooperation and undeclared nuclear sites prompted Tehran to announce new advanced centrifuges, escalating tensions rather than signaling compromise. With Iran's near-weapons-grade stockpile growing and no diplomatic breakthroughs, markets reflect low expectations for a pre-deadline deal reverting to JCPOA limits.

events.title: CA Paranaense vs. Botafogo FR
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Brazil Série A game, scheduled for Wednesday, March 11, 2026 between CA Paranaense and Botafogo FR.

eventMetadata.context_description: Athletico Paranaense's formidable home record at Arena da Baixada drives their edge in trader consensus for this Serie A showdown, remaining unbeaten in five straight matches there amid strong recent form (three wins in last five overall). Botafogo, Serie A leaders with an 11-game unbeaten streak, face headwinds from midfielder Marlon Freitas' suspension and defender Ricardo Silva's injury, potentially disrupting their midfield control on the road where they've dropped points lately. The high draw probability mirrors four level results in the last six head-to-heads, with both sides prioritizing points in the title and mid-table races—Paranaense's full attacking lineup per latest reports further supports the status quo odds.

question: Will Elon Musk be confirmed to have visited Epstein’s island?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if evidence is made public confirming that the listed individual previously visited Little St. James by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

This includes but is not limited to flight logs, photographs, videos, court records, or sworn testimony that clearly place the listed individual on the island. Public confirmation from the listed individual that they visited Little St. James will also qualify for a “Yes” resolution.

If any release of Epstein-related files is carried out by the United States federal government within 48 hours of this market's resolution time, this market will remain open for 48 hours after the resolution time to allow for evidence of visits to Little St. James to be investigated.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket's Epstein Island visitor market favors low probabilities for most high-profile names, reflecting the absence of a comprehensive, verified visitor list from Little Saint James despite January 2024 unsealed court documents from the Giuffre-Maxwell case naming associates like Bill Clinton and Donald Trump without confirming island visits. Flight logs provide partial evidence of travel to the property, but denials from implicated figures and lack of direct proof dominate sentiment. Recent developments include Trump's August 2024 pledge to declassify Epstein files if reelected, fueling speculation, while no new primary disclosures have emerged. Ongoing civil suits could yield more documents, though timelines remain uncertain amid legal appeals.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if evidence is made public confirming that the listed individual previously visited Little St. James by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

This includes but is not limited to flight logs, photographs, videos, court records, or sworn testimony that clearly place the listed individual on the island. Public confirmation from the listed individual that they visited Little St. James will also qualify for a “Yes” resolution.

If any release of Epstein-related files is carried out by the United States federal government within 48 hours of this market's resolution time, this market will remain open for 48 hours after the resolution time to allow for evidence of visits to Little St. James to be investigated.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket's Epstein Island visitor market favors low probabilities for most high-profile names, reflecting the absence of a comprehensive, verified visitor list from Little Saint James despite January 2024 unsealed court documents from the Giuffre-Maxwell case naming associates like Bill Clinton and Donald Trump without confirming island visits. Flight logs provide partial evidence of travel to the property, but denials from implicated figures and lack of direct proof dominate sentiment. Recent developments include Trump's August 2024 pledge to declassify Epstein files if reelected, fueling speculation, while no new primary disclosures have emerged. Ongoing civil suits could yield more documents, though timelines remain uncertain amid legal appeals.
